title: lophira lanceolata
intro: Lophira lanceolata, also known as the African walnut, is a tropical tree native to the rainforests of West and Central Africa. The bark and leaves of the plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Lophira lanceolata include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional African medicine for its various therapeutic applications.
